title: 10 best herbal essential oils for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for oily hair are a natural solution to manage excessive oil production, reducing greasiness and promoting healthy locks. These potent oils are derived from plants and herbs, which help balance the scalp's natural pH level, reducing sebum secretion and leaving hair looking fresh and clean. Lavender, tea tree, and rosemary essential oils are popular choices for oily hair, as they have antiseptic and astringent properties that absorb excess oil and purify the scalp. With regular use, these oils can significantly improve the lives of individuals with oily hair, freeing them from frequent shampooing and styling struggles, and allowing them to enjoy a more manageable, healthy-looking mane.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. When added to a shampoo or used as a final rinse, it helps to balance the scalp's pH level and reduces excessive oil production. It also has the ability to combat dandruff and itchiness, which can contribute to oily locks. Additionally, tea tree essential oils possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe an irritated scalp, leaving hair looking healthy and manageable with reduced greasiness.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Help to combat scalp infections and reduce oil production by targeting the source of the problem, reducing flakiness and itchiness associated with oily scalps.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Soothe and calm irritated skin and scalp, reducing redness and inflammation caused by clogged pores and excess sebum, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Astringent properties: Help to balance the scalp's natural pH level, tightening the pores and reducing oil production, leaving hair looking cleaner and feeling lighter.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cineole, a compound that helps to reduce sebum production and combat dandruff.
Roots: Known for their antibacterial properties, which can help to control fungal infections that contribute to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the early morning for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in small bunches using air circulation or shade drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a clevenger apparatus with a ratio of 1:4 leaf material to water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a glass separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.
Mix 10 drops of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil like jojoba or coconut oil for hair application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The oil absorbs excess sebum, reducing greasiness and leaving hair looking healthier and more manageable. Additionally, the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of English lavender essential oils help to soothe an itchy scalp and prevent inflammation, which can contribute to oily hair. Regular use of this natural remedy can lead to a balanced hair texture and a reduction in oil production.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: English Lavender essential oil's antimicrobial property helps to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by causing irritation and flaking.

Balancing and regulating sebum production: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to balance and regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oil secretion and helping to normalize the natural oil production process.

Anti-inflammatory property: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can lead to oily hair by causing irritation and redness, while also soothing and calming the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its grounding and balancing properties, promoting healthy scalp and hair growth.
Flowers: Known for their calming and antiseptic effects, helping to reduce oil production and soothe irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers during full bloom for optimal oil yield.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Place 50g of dried flowers into a steam distillation apparatus and heat it to 100°c for 4-6 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil from the condenser coil and transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage.
Allow the extracted oil to mature for at least 24 hours before using or storing it in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with oily hair because its antiseptic and astringent properties help to reduce excess oil production in the scalp. The oil's ability to stimulate blood flow also improves circulation, which in turn helps to regulate sebum production. Additionally, rosemary's natural antibacterial properties help to combat bacteria that can contribute to greasiness. Overall, using rosemary essential oils as a hair treatment or adding it to your shampoo can help to balance the scalp and reduce oily hair.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Rosemary essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff, by reducing the amount of excess sebum produced.
Astringent property: Rosemary essential oil's astringent nature helps to dry out and reduce the size of pores, minimizing oil production and preventing clogged follicles that can lead to oily hair.

Stimulant property: Rosemary essential oil's stimulant properties help increase blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff and flakiness, which can exacerbate oily hair issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosemary leaves are used to make essential oil, which helps to balance sebum production and reduce dandruff in oily hair.
Stems: The stems of rosemary are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in controlling oiliness and soothing irritated scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves, stems and flowers, ensure at least 20% of leaves are intact.
Dry the plant material using air circulation method or shade drying for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a still with a water-to-material ratio of 1:2, operate for 4 hours and collect 15ml of essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities, store in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil at room temperature away from direct sunlight, use within 6 months for best efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. It also contains antioxidants that combat the free radicals that can contribute to excessive oil secretion. Additionally, thyme essential oil has a gentle, non-comedogenic formula that won't clog pores or irritate the scalp, allowing it to effectively regulate oiliness and leave hair looking healthy, shiny, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial properties help to control the growth of bacteria on the scalp, reducing excessive oil production and preventing clogged pores.
Astringent property: The astringent property of thyme essential oil helps to shrink the size of pores, tightening the hair follicles and reducing the appearance of oily roots.

Antifungal property: Thyme essential oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections that can cause excessive oil production and irritation on the scalp, leading to a healthier and more balanced scalp.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves contain high amounts of antioxidants and antibacterial properties, making them effective in controlling oil production and purifying the scalp.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have astringent properties that help to absorb excess oil and reduce sebum production, leaving hair feeling lighter and cleaner.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from your garden or purchase high-quality dried thyme.
Dry the harvested thyme leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried thyme leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Combine crushed thyme with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at 1:5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking occasionally before straining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with oily hair because of its remarkable properties that balance sebum production. The oil's antiseptic and antibacterial qualities help to purify the scalp, reducing inflammation and excess oil secretion. Additionally, geranium oil's natural astringent properties tighten the pores, preventing dirt and impurities from clogging them, thereby controlling oiliness and leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. Regular use of geranium essential oils can lead to a balanced scalp and reduced oiliness, making it an excellent addition to any hair care routine.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Geranium essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair, scalp issues, and dandruff.

Balancing property: Geranium essential oil has a balancing effect on the body's hormonal system, which can help regulate sebum production in the hair follicles, reducing excess oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.

Antiseptic property: Geranium essential oil's antiseptic properties can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reduce inflammation, and prevent further irritation and infection that can exacerbate oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Geranium are used to make essential oils that help to balance sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp.
Seeds: Geranium seeds are used to create essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe itchy scalps and reduce flakiness associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of pelargonium graveolens at dawn and dry them in shade for three hours.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and place one cup of them in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil to the jar with the chopped leaves.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, retaining the liquid as your herbal essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to balance the scalp's natural moisture levels, reducing excess oil production. Additionally, sage oil's astringent properties help to tighten the pores on the scalp, allowing for less sebum buildup and a cleaner, healthier hair environment. This results in a reduction of oily residue, leaving hair looking healthy, manageable, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals in the scalp, which can contribute to excessive oil production and dandruff.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage essential oil help to constrict pores and reduce sebum secretion, leading to a decrease in oily hair and scalp conditions.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an excellent choice for reducing inflammation and soothing scalp irritations associated with oily hair.
Buds: Sage buds contain concentrated amounts of salvene, a compound that helps to regulate sebum production and reduce oiliness, leaving hair feeling clean and balanced.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh salvia sclarea leaves on a sunny day at peak oil production.
Wash the harvested leaves in lukewarm water and mild soap to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves using 500ml of distilled water for 4 hours and 15 minutes.
Collect the extracted essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or paper towel.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow essential oils helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. It absorbs excess sebum, reducing greasiness and leaving hair looking cleaner and healthier. The oils also stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy follicles and a reduction in oil production. As a result, yarrow essential oils can help to control oiliness, absorb impurities, and leave hair feeling light, refreshed, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Yarrow essential oil helps to reduce sebum production, drying out excess oil on the scalp and hair, leading to a healthier balance of moisture and oil.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Yarrow essential oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to prevent fungal infections and bacteria growth that can contribute to oily hair, scalp irritation, and dandruff.

Anti-inflammatory property: Yarrow essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with oily hair, such as acne and irritations caused by excessive product buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, yarrow roots can help regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation associated with oily hair.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, yarrow flowers can help balance the scalp's pH level, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in abundance, taking care not to contaminate with soil or debris.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it thin on a paper towel or clean cloth for several days.
Steam distill 500g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers using 1 liter of water for 2-3 hours, collecting 15ml of oil.
Filter the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities, discarding any sediment or residue.
Bottle the clear essential oil in dark glass containers, labeling with date, ingredients, and intended use as an herbal treatment for oily hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with oily hair because its antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control the growth of bacteria and fungus that can contribute to an oily scalp. Additionally, sage oil's astringent nature helps to absorb excess oil and reduce sebum production, leaving your hair feeling lighter and cleaner. Its purifying qualities also help to remove impurities and product buildup from the hair shaft, allowing for healthier-looking locks with reduced oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage oil helps control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, reducing excessive oil production and preventing infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated scalps and reduce redness and swelling caused by excess oil and product buildup.
Antioxidant properties: Sage oil contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect the hair follicles from damage caused by environmental stressors, UV rays, and pollution.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in controlling sebum production and soothing irritated scalps.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat scalp infections that can contribute to oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning for optimal oil content retention.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least 7 days to reduce moisture levels.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered herb with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil and steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids before storing in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The oil's antiseptic and antibacterial properties also work to combat dandruff and other scalp irritations that can contribute to excessive oiliness. Additionally, marjoram oil has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy hair growth. By using a few drops of sweet marjoram essential oils in your shampoo or conditioner, you can say goodbye to greasy locks and hello to a healthier, more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sweet marjoram essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help to control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by reducing its ability to produce natural oils.

Astringent properties: The astringent properties of sweet marjoram essential oil help to dry up excess sebum production, tightening the pores and absorbing excess oil, resulting in a more balanced scalp and less greasy hair.

Antioxidant properties: Sweet marjoram essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp and hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair by weakening the scalp's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their balancing properties, marjoram leaves help regulate sebum production and reduce dandruff in oily hair.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, marjoram flowers soothe the scalp and promote healthy hair growth while controlling oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh marjoram leaves and flowers on a sunny morning for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours or air dry for 7 days.
Blend 10g of dried marjoram with 100ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil and let it sit for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the essential oils from the solids after 14 days.
Filter the resulting liquid using a paper filter or cotton cloth to remove any remaining impurities before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with oily hair because of its remarkable ability to balance the scalp's natural oil production. The oil's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to purify the scalp, reducing excess sebum secretion and preventing clogged pores. Additionally, the oil's astringent properties absorb excess oil and tighten the hair follicles, leaving hair looking clean, fresh, and manageable. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils provide long-lasting relief from oily hair, leaving it with a healthy shine and a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum Essential Oil's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair by breaking down sebum and causing irritation.

Decongestant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the scalp, which can alleviate itchiness and flakiness associated with oily hair.

Astringent and Antioxidant Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum Essential Oil's astringent properties help to dry out the scalp and reduce oil production, while its antioxidant properties protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to regulate sebum production and reduce dandruff.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks have antibacterial properties that combat scalp infections and prevent excessive oil buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the collected leaves with distilled water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a paper filter or coffee filter carefully.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal essential oils is to dilute them in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and massage into the scalp. Use a blend of tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile oil to balance sebum production and reduce dandruff. Apply a warm compress after application to stimulate blood flow and promote healthy hair growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal essential oils are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These skin conditions often share similarities with oily hair in terms of excess oil production, clogged pores, and irritation. Herbal essential oils like tea tree, lavender, and chamomile can help balance skin and scalp oils, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin and hair growth.
